architecture.summary: A two-layer transformer that performs chain-of-continuous-thought (CoCoT) reasoning on directed graph reachability. The model autoregressively generates continuous thought vectors (dense embeddings rather than discrete tokens) at each reasoning step. Each continuous thought is constructed as a superposition state encoding multiple reachable vertices simultaneously via normalized linear combinations. Layer 1 attention uses five 'attention chooser' heads to copy source and target nodes from edges onto edge tokens; Layer 2 attention expands the frontier by attending to outgoing edges from currently reachable nodes and accumulating target nodes back into the superposition. MLPs filter noise and normalize weights. At the final step, an answer token measures the superposition to select the reachable candidate.
trained_components: attention layers (query, key, value, output matrices)

biblio.title: Reasoning by Superposition: A Theoretical Perspective on Chain of Continuous Thought

baseline.name: constant-depth transformers with discrete CoT (Merrill and Sabharwal 2023a)

claims.statement: a two-layer transformer with D steps of continuous CoTs can solve the directed graph reachability problem, where D is the diameter of the graph
the best known result of constant-depth transformers with discrete CoTs requires O(n²) decoding steps where n is the number of vertices, while continuous CoTs require D steps (D < n)

each continuous thought vector is a superposition state that encodes multiple search frontiers simultaneously (parallel breadth-first search), while discrete CoTs must choose a single path sampled from the superposition state
a 2-layer transformer trained from scratch with Coconut achieves near-perfect accuracy on ProsQA, while both CoT and No CoT baselines solve only about 75% of the tasks
a 12-layer transformer with discrete CoT achieves 83% accuracy on ProsQA, still unable to solve the tasks reliably
the model's Layer 1 attention instantiates a copying mechanism that places nearly all attention mass on source and target nodes of edges, confirming the theoretical construction

the model's Layer 2 attention sharply concentrates on reachable edges and exhibits bias toward the frontier subset (nodes exactly D steps from root), with additional emphasis on optimal edges leading to the solution

encoding of multiple search frontiers as a superposition state automatically emerges in training continuous CoTs, without explicit supervision to guide the model to explore multiple paths simultaneously (demonstrated by Coconut-BFS achieving similar performance to supervised Coconut)
